perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date hereof, neither it nor any of its affiliates will solicit for employment, directly or indirectly, any member of the other Party's senior management with whom it first had contact, or who was specifically identified to it, during the period of its evaluation of the Transaction; provided, however, that this paragraph will not prevent either Party from -------- ------- employing any person who contacts such Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such Party. For purposes of this paragraph, "solicit for employment" shall not be deemed to include any general solicitations of employment by one Party not specifically directed towards employees of the other Party. Nothing herein shall prevent either Party from, directly or indirectly, soliciting for employment or hiring any member of the other Party's senior management whose employment with such other Party has been terminated.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date hereof, neither it nor any of its affiliates will solicit for employment, directly or indirectly, any member of the other Party's senior management with whom it first had contact, or who was specifically identified to it, during the period of its evaluation of the Transaction; provided, however, that this paragraph will not prevent either Party from -------- ------- employing any person who contacts such Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the explicit definition of 'solicit for employment,' the scope of the restriction is now ambiguous. The later statement that 'Nothing herein shall prevent either Party from, directly or indirectly, soliciting for employment or hiring any member of the other Party's senior management whose employment with such other Party has been terminated' in section 4 becomes contradictory. With the removed definition, it's unclear if actively recruiting already terminated employees constitutes a violation of the non-solicitation agreement, making the paragraph as a whole unclear and difficult to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Each Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date hereof, neither it nor any of its affiliates will solicit for employment, directly or indirectly, any member of the other Party's senior management with whom it first had contact, or who was specifically identified to it, during the period of its evaluation of the Transaction; provided, however, that this paragraph will not prevent either Party from -------- ------- employing any person who contacts such Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such Party. Nothing herein shall prevent either Party from, directly or indirectly, soliciting for employment or hiring any member of the other Party's senior management whose employment with such other Party has been terminated.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'solicit for employment,' which provides clarity to the non-solicitation agreement. The changed text removes this definition, creating ambiguity. The contradicted text, which is identical to the original, relies on this definition to determine whether specific actions, such as hiring terminated employees, are permissible. Without the definition, it's unclear whether actively recruiting already terminated employees falls under the non-solicitation clause, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party at any time, shall promptly return or destroy all such tangible Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in its possession, and the Receiving Party shall thereafter retain no such Confidential Information in any form. The Receiving Party shall be fully responsible for the return or destruction of all Confidential Information disclosed to its affiliates, its employees, it representatives and/or its consultants.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party at any time, shall promptly return or destroy all such tangible Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in its possession, and the Receiving Party shall thereafter retain no such Confidential Information in any form.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'The Receiving Party shall be fully responsible for the return or destruction of all Confidential Information disclosed to its affiliates, its employees, it representatives and/or its consultants.', the contract lacks clarity on who is accountable for the confidential information held by third parties. Section 2 states: 'The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to its affiliates, its employees, its representatives and its consultants...', and now if that information is not returned or destroyed, the Receiving Party can say that they don't have accountability of the information because the clause which said they were fully responsible has been removed. It creates a contradiction because it becomes vague about the Receiving Party accountability of the third parties that received the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Receiving Party shall use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and for the purposes of the ongoing Transaction, and shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to third parties. The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to its affiliates, its employees, its representatives and its consultants, in each case if such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants have a need to know, and providing such affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants (i) use the Confidential Information for the purposes of the Transaction only, and (ii) are bound to protect the Confidential Information to the same extent as the Receiving Party is bound. The Parties shall each be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by them or their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants and hereby agree, at their sole expense, to take all reasonable measures (including but not limited to court proceedings) to restrain their respective affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information. The term "affiliate" means any person or entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with a Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the clause stating the Receiving Party is responsible for the return/destruction of Confidential Information disclosed to its affiliates, employees, etc. This contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that the Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its affiliates, employees, etc., and that the parties shall each be responsible for any breach of the terms of the Agreement by them or their respective affiliates, employees, etc. The removal of the responsibility clause creates a contradiction because the Receiving Party can now argue they are not responsible for the actions of their affiliates, employees, etc., regarding the confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Dimension hereby acknowledges that Proxicom is disclosing its Confidential Information in consideration of Dimension's agreement not to propose to Proxicom or any other person or entity any transaction between Dimension and Proxicom and/or its security holders or involving any of Proxicom's securities or security holders unless Proxicom shall have requested in writing that Dimension make such a proposal, and that Dimension will not acquire, or assist, advise or encourage any other persons or entities in acquiring, directly or indirectly, control of Proxicom or any of Proxicom's securities, businesses or assets for a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement unless Proxicom shall have consented in advance in writing to such acquisition. Dimension also agrees that Proxicom shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this paragraph and Dimension further agrees that it shall not oppose the granting of such relief. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Dimension shall not be subject to the restrictions provided in this paragraph 9 if (i) at any time, any person or entity other than Dimension announces an intention to acquire, directly or indirectly, any voting 2 <PAGE> securities or assets of Proxicom or (ii) Proxicom publicly solicits offers for its acquisition or the acquisition of its business and/or assets in their entirety.
perturbation.changed_text: Dimension hereby acknowledges that Proxicom is disclosing its Confidential Information in consideration of Dimension's agreement not to propose to Proxicom or any other person or entity any transaction between Dimension and Proxicom and/or its security holders or involving any of Proxicom's securities or security holders unless Proxicom shall have requested in writing that Dimension make such a proposal, and that Dimension will not acquire, or assist, advise or encourage any other persons or entities in acquiring, directly or indirectly, control of Proxicom or any of Proxicom's securities, businesses or assets for a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement unless Proxicom shall have consented in advance in writing to such acquisition. Dimension also agrees that Proxicom shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this paragraph and Dimension further agrees that it shall not oppose the granting of such relief.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the section 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, Dimension shall not be subject to the restrictions provided in this paragraph 9 if (i) at any time, any person or entity other than Dimension announces an intention to acquire, directly or indirectly, any voting 2 <PAGE> securities or assets of Proxicom or (ii) Proxicom publicly solicits offers for its acquisition or the acquisition of its business and/or assets in their entirety.', the safe harbor provisions are eliminated. This omission creates a contradiction. While the paragraph initially imposes restrictions on Dimension regarding acquiring Proxicom's assets, businesses, or securities, it doesn't now provide the exception to the restrictions provided in this paragraph 9. Now, regardless of another company intending to acquire Proxicom or if Proxicom solicits other offers, Dimension is still subject to the original agreement. This directly contradicts the implied freedom to act in response to external acquisition intentions or public solicitations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Dimension hereby acknowledges that Proxicom is disclosing its Confidential Information in consideration of Dimension's agreement not to propose to Proxicom or any other person or entity any transaction between Dimension and Proxicom and/or its security holders or involving any of Proxicom's securities or security holders unless Proxicom shall have requested in writing that Dimension make such a proposal, and that Dimension will not acquire, or assist, advise or encourage any other persons or entities in acquiring, directly or indirectly, control of Proxicom or any of Proxicom's securities, businesses or assets for a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement unless Proxicom shall have consented in advance in writing to such acquisition. Dimension also agrees that Proxicom shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this paragraph and Dimension further agrees that it shall not oppose the granting of such relief.
perturbation.justification: The change removes an exception clause from paragraph 9 regarding Dimension's restrictions on acquiring Proxicom's assets. The original text provided an exception to the restrictions if another entity announced an intention to acquire Proxicom, or if Proxicom solicited offers for its acquisition. By removing this exception, the changed text creates a contradiction. Now, even if such events occur, Dimension remains bound by the original restrictions, which contradicts the implied freedom to act in response to external acquisition intentions or public solicitations that was initially provided for.

perturbation.original_text: To the extent that any Confidential Information may include materials subject to the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or any other applicable privilege concerning pending or threatened legal proceedings or governmental investigations, the Parties understand and agree that they have a commonality of interest with respect to such matters and it is their respective desire, intention and mutual understanding that the sharing of such material is not intended to, and shall not, waive or diminish in any way the confidentiality of such material or its continued protection under the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or other applicable privilege. All Confidential Information that is entitled to protection under the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine and other applicable privilege shall remain entitled to such protection under these privileges, this Agreement, and under the joint defense doctrine.
perturbation.changed_text: To the extent that any Confidential Information may include materials subject to the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or any other applicable privilege concerning pending or threatened legal proceedings or governmental investigations, the Parties understand and agree that they have a commonality of interest with respect to such matters and it is their respective desire, intention and mutual understanding that the sharing of such material is not intended to, and shall not, waive or diminish in any way the confidentiality of such material or its continued protection under the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or other applicable privilege.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'All Confidential Information that is entitled to protection under the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine and other applicable privilege shall remain entitled to such protection under these privileges, this Agreement, and under the joint defense doctrine', the agreement lacks enforceability related to privileged information. The beginning of the paragraph says, 'To the extent that any Confidential Information may include materials subject to the attorney-client privilege', and by omitting the last sentence, there is no statement indicating that the materials shall remain entitled to such protection under these privileges. The removal of the last sentence create an in-text contradiction, because the beginning of the sentence introduces the idea that confidential information may be subject to attorney-client privilege, but fails to indicate it will maintain such.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. To the extent that any Confidential Information may include materials subject to the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or any other applicable privilege concerning pending or threatened legal proceedings or governmental investigations, the Parties understand and agree that they have a commonality of interest with respect to such matters and it is their respective desire, intention and mutual understanding that the sharing of such material is not intended to, and shall not, waive or diminish in any way the confidentiality of such material or its continued protection under the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or other applicable privilege.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that Confidential Information entitled to protection under attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine, and other applicable privileges shall remain entitled to such protection under these privileges, this Agreement, and under the joint defense doctrine. The changed text removes this explicit guarantee, creating a contradiction, because without the final sentence in the original text, there is no guarantee that privileged information will remain protected, contradicting the contradicted text (which is the original text itself, since the changed text omits a part of it) and leaving room for uncertainty or a waiver of privilege. Thus, it is a strong in-text contradiction.
